evals.prompt: Please do a code quality review of the current changes.
evals.expected_output: Infers the review scope from git state (uncommitted working-tree diff, else main...HEAD / master...HEAD) per the scope ladder, or returns NEEDS SCOPE and asks for a target when nothing is reviewable. Does not invent findings or edit files.
assertions: Determines review scope from git state (uncommitted diff first, otherwise main...HEAD or master...HEAD) or returns NEEDS SCOPE asking for a specific target
Does not fabricate findings about code it has not actually seen
Does not edit, format, or commit any files

evals.prompt: 帮我审查一下 order_service.ts 的代码质量，重点看可维护性和结构问题。
evals.expected_output: Chinese-language review that flags the any-typed boundary with silent fallback plus at least one other structural issue, using stable CQ-IDs, without editing the fixture.
assertions: The review is written in Chinese
Flags the `any` typing and silent `?? {}` fallback as a boundary/type-contract issue
Flags at least one additional structural issue (mode-based branching with duplicated coupon logic, the thin findOrder wrapper, the duplicated formatMoney, or the non-atomic charge/update)
Findings use stable IDs such as CQ-001
Does not edit order_service.ts

evals.prompt: Review order_service.ts for code quality issues and suggest improvements.
evals.expected_output: Structured review following the required format: a Verdict line, findings with stable CQ-IDs each carrying Location, Evidence, Why it matters, Recommended remediation, and Confidence. Catches the non-atomic payment/order update and the any/silent-fallback boundary.
assertions: Output includes a Verdict line (REQUEST CHANGES | COMMENT ONLY | NEEDS SCOPE | NO MAJOR FINDINGS)
Findings use stable IDs (CQ-001, CQ-002, ...)
Each finding includes Location, Evidence, Why it matters, Recommended remediation, and Confidence
Identifies the non-atomic payment/order update where charge can succeed while the order is never marked paid
Identifies the `any` type-boundary and silent empty-object fallback
Does not edit order_service.ts

evals.prompt: Can you review the code quality here? I have not attached a patch or file path.
evals.expected_output: With no reviewable target and no usable git diff, returns NEEDS SCOPE and asks for the exact files, diff, PR, patch, or subsystem instead of inventing findings.
assertions: Returns NEEDS SCOPE (or clearly asks for a target) rather than producing findings
Asks for specific files, a diff, a PR, a patch, or a subsystem
Does not fabricate findings or edit any files

evals.prompt: Do a code review of order_service.ts focused on maintainability. Do not change anything yet.
evals.expected_output: Read-only maintainability review: analyzes and recommends structural improvements without editing the fixture or any file.
assertions: Performs the analysis without editing order_service.ts or any other file
Provides recommendations rather than applying refactors
Includes at least one structural finding with a concrete remediation

evals.prompt: Review order_service.ts for code quality and save the report so my team can audit it later.
evals.expected_output: Activates the opt-in artifact mode: writes the review to code_review/<feature>/quality-review.md (a review artifact under code_review/, never product code), with a Verdict and CQ-ID findings, and recommends gitignoring code_review/ without editing .gitignore itself.
assertions: Creates a review artifact file under code_review/ (e.g. code_review/<feature>/quality-review.md)
Does NOT modify order_service.ts
The saved report contains a Verdict and findings with stable CQ-IDs
Recommends adding code_review/ to .gitignore without editing .gitignore unless asked
